name: Gougeres (Cheese Puffs)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. In a small saucepan, combine water, butter, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil.
3. Remove from heat immediately and add flour all at once. Stir until a ball forms.
4. Return mixture to medium heat and dry the paste by stirring for about 5 minutes, or until butter starts oozing in bubbles.
5. The paste should not stick to your fingers when pinched.
6. Allow the paste to cool slightly (about 5 minutes), then beat in eggs one at a time, waiting for each egg to be fully incorporated before adding the next.
7. Add grated Gruyere cheese and Dijon mustard.
8. Butter a baking sheet or line with parchment paper.
9. Pipe small rounds (about 1/2-inch in diameter) onto the prepared sheet, leaving space between them.
10. Brush each puff with beaten egg or milk and sprinkle additional cheese on top.
11. Bake for 15 minutes at 425°F, then lower heat to 400°F and bake an additional 10 minutes.
12. Allow to cool on a wire rack before serving.
(Note: Gougeres freeze well.)
